content:
## MARKET OVERVIEW

1. What is the Current Market Regime?
RISK-ON: Normal volatility environment with broad participation. Vol: 26.5%, Breadth: 7/8 sectors positive.

2. Which sectors are leading?
Energy & Chemicals is the clear leader: TUPRS (1W: +6.70% | 1M: +20.50%) ABOVE TREND; PETKM (1W: +13.56% | 1M: +4.49%) ABOVE TREND. Holding & Investment and Technology & Telecom follow: DOHOL (1W: +5.59% | 1M: -2.49%) RECOVERY; KONTR (1W: +6.29% | 1M: -19.80%) RECOVERY.

3. Which sectors are lagging?
Banking & Finance is the laggard on the week despite breadth: GARAN (1W: +1.03% | 1M: -14.61%) RECOVERY; AKBNK (1W: +0.82% | 1M: -16.35%) RECOVERY. Retail & Food also under pressure: SOKM (1W: -8.11% | 1M: -20.83%) BELOW TREND; ULKER (1W: -3.93% | 1M: -19.93%) BELOW TREND.

4. Top Performing Stocks?
SASA (1W: +14.22% | 1M: -7.78%) RECOVERY; PETKM (1W: +13.56% | 1M: +4.49%) ABOVE TREND; TKFEN (1W: +9.56% | 1M: +2.00%) ABOVE TREND; TSKB (1W: +8.11% | 1M: -11.65%) RECOVERY; BIMAS (1W: +7.82% | 1M: -2.54%) RECOVERY.

5. Worst Performing Stocks?
HALKB (1W: -14.58% | 1M: -17.08%) BELOW TREND; SOKM (1W: -8.11% | 1M: -20.83%) BELOW TREND; VAKBN (1W: -7.74% | 1M: -21.59%) BELOW TREND; ULKER (1W: -3.93% | 1M: -19.93%) BELOW TREND; EKGYO (1W: -2.76% | 1M: -20.17%) BELOW TREND.

6. Momentum Stocks?
Biggest 1W movers are SASA (1W: +14.22% | 1M: -7.78%) RECOVERY and PETKM (1W: +13.56% | 1M: +4.49%) ABOVE TREND, followed by TKFEN (1W: +9.56% | 1M: +2.00%) ABOVE TREND, TSKB (1W: +8.11% | 1M: -11.65%) RECOVERY and SISE (1W: +7.69% | 1M: -11.79%) RECOVERY.

7. Banking Sector Outlook?
Banks show short-term recovery but structural weakness: ISCTR (1W: +1.99% | 1M: -19.88%) RECOVERY and GARAN (1W: +1.03% | 1M: -14.61%) RECOVERY are bouncing, while HALKB (1W: -14.58% | 1M: -17.08%) BELOW TREND and VAKBN (1W: -7.74% | 1M: -21.59%) BELOW TREND remain weak. Sector underweight or selective exposure recommended until 1M trends confirm.

8. Industrial Sector Outlook?
Heavy industry mixed with selective strength in autos and defense: TOASO (1W: +7.27% | 1M: -8.42%) RECOVERY and ASELS (1W: +1.33% | 1M: +6.95%) ABOVE TREND lead, while EREGL (1W: +0.64% | 1M: -3.65%) RECOVERY lags. Construction/REIT names remain under pressure: ALARK (1W: -1.89% | 1M: -20.12%) BELOW TREND; EKGYO (1W: -2.76% | 1M: -20.17%) BELOW TREND.

9. Energy & Chemicals Outlook?
Sector momentum is strong and broad: TUPRS (1W: +6.70% | 1M: +20.50%) ABOVE TREND and PETKM (1W: +13.56% | 1M: +4.49%) ABOVE TREND are primary plays, with SASA (1W: +14.22% | 1M: -7.78%) RECOVERY providing volatile upside. Watch TRMET (1W: -0.61% | 1M: +4.28%) CORRECTION for profit-taking dynamics.

10. Portfolio Recommendation?
Risk-on stance: overweight Energy & Chemicals and selective heavy-industrials (TUPRS, PETKM, TKFEN) while trimming cyclically weak retail and some regional banks. Keep liquidity to rotate on macro headlines (USD/TRY, oil, global rates) and tighten stops on names in BELOW TREND.

11. Stocks to Watch Next Week?
TUPRS (1W: +6.70% | 1M: +20.50%) ABOVE TREND; PETKM (1W: +13.56% | 1M: +4.49%) ABOVE TREND; SASA (1W: +14.22% | 1M: -7.78%) RECOVERY; TKFEN (1W: +9.56% | 1M: +2.00%) ABOVE TREND; HALKB (1W: -14.58% | 1M: -17.08%) BELOW TREND.

12. Sectors to Watch Next Week?
Energy & Chemicals for momentum and earnings flow, and Banking & Finance for risk sentiment and FX/capital moves. Also monitor Technology & Telecom for sentiment-driven rebounds.

13. Key Levels & Triggers?
Monitor XU100 for continuation above recent multi-week highs (confirm RISK-ON) and for breakdown below the last pullback low (signal rotation to caution); watch catalysts: USD/TRY moves, weekly oil price action, domestic CPI/macro prints and global rate headlines. Earnings and sector-specific news (TUPRS/PETKM releases, bank regulatory updates) will be primary triggers.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
RISK-ON market with normal volatility and broad sector participation; Energy & Chemicals lead while banks remain the weakest link. Trade overweight in TUPRS/PETKM and selective industrials, keep stops tight on BELOW TREND retail and regional banks and watch macro FX and oil catalysts for next rotation.
